key_insight: SimPhysNet predicts laser weld penetration with high accuracy by combining physics-informed self-supervised feature learning with few-shot classification, reducing dependence on large labeled datasets.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more practical quality control for laser welding, supporting intelligent automation and defect reduction in industrial manufacturing.
method: Physics-informed neural network learns physically meaningful molten-pool and keyhole features from unlabeled images
Three image augmentation tasks strengthen contrastive self-supervised representation learning
Prototypical networks classify penetration state using only a small labeled subset
evidence_signal: The model achieved 96.06% classification accuracy using only 200 labeled images, about 5% of the labeled dataset, comparable to fully supervised methods.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of unlabeled welding images and on how well the learned physical priors transfer to new welding setups.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
